title: introduce-policy-contracts-kms-commitment-ledger-and-residency-gate
preview: Context and motivation: Customers operating under jurisdictional controls asked for stronger, auditable guarantees that (1) requests that claim a region-bound residency are actually served with region-anchored CMKs, (2) operator principles cannot bypass tenant-scoped RBAC policies, and (3) audit trails contain verif...
text: Context and motivation: Customers operating under jurisdictional controls asked for stronger, auditable guarantees that (1) requests that claim a region-bound residency are actually served with region-anchored CMKs, (2) operator principles cannot bypass tenant-scoped RBAC policies, and (3) audit trails contain verifiable cryptographic markers tying requests to KMS commitments. This PR implements three coordinated pieces: (A) a lightweight Policy Contracts DSL and compiler that lets product and compliance own high-level contracts (e.g., "Tenant must use CMK in eu-west-1 for classification=restricted") and compiles into RBACv2 assertions and enforcer rules; (B) a KMS Commitment Ledger service that records authoritative, append-only KMS assignment decisions per request (signed by the enforcer) and surfaces a compact proof token that is attached to audit events; (C) a Residency Gate enfor...

title: Add Optimization Config change permissions + operator roles (apply/promote/rollback)
preview: Summary: Introduces explicit RBAC permissions for the Optimization Config lifecycle (create/validate/apply/promote/rollback) and adds two opinionated roles used by Console + control plane workflows. This is part of the Safe apply + rollback mechanism project to ensure rollback is both auditable and access-controlled...
text: Summary: Introduces explicit RBAC permissions for the Optimization Config lifecycle (create/validate/apply/promote/rollback) and adds two opinionated roles used by Console + control plane workflows. This is part of the Safe apply + rollback mechanism project to ensure rollback is both auditable and access-controlled. Motivation: Today, optimization config changes are implicitly covered by broad “platform operator” capabilities in some environments, and not consistently modeled in Private deployments. Enterprise customers and Security review require least-privilege roles with clear separation between “can propose” and “can execute rollback in prod.” Changes included: 1) New permissions: optimization_config.read, optimization_config.create_version, optimization_config.validate, optimization_config.diff, optimization_config.apply, optimization_config.promote, optimization_config.rollback...

title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations
preview: Title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations. Context: As part of offline bundle signature verification, the installer now supports a policy-gated break-glass flag (see redwood-private-installer pr-24979). This PR adds cluster-side guardrails so that (1) break-glass cannot be ...
text: Title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations. Context: As part of offline bundle signature verification, the installer now supports a policy-gated break-glass flag (see redwood-private-installer pr-24979). This PR adds cluster-side guardrails so that (1) break-glass cannot be enabled silently, (2) use is attributable and exportable via audit logs, and (3) only approved operators can authorize it. Summary of changes: (A) Adds a Gatekeeper ConstraintTemplate + Constraint enforcing required annotations whenever break-glass is enabled via the Redwood Private install metadata ConfigMap (redwood-private/install-metadata) or Helm values rendered into that ConfigMap. Required annotations: redwood.ai/break-glass-ticket (SUP-/INT-/customer ticket id), redwood.ai/break-glass-reason (freeform, min 20 chars), redwood.ai/break-glass-approved-by (corporate ema...

title: Add least-privilege RBAC for release signing service operator
preview:
## Summary
Introduce a dedicated, least-privilege RBAC role for "release signing operators" who need to initiate and monitor offline bundle signing runs for Redwood Private releases.

This PR removes the current ad-hoc guidance of granting broad permissions in the release-tools cluster and replaces it with an explic...
text:
## Summary
Introduce a dedicated, least-privilege RBAC role for "release signing operators" who need to initiate and monitor offline bundle signing runs for Redwood Private releases.

This PR removes the current ad-hoc guidance of granting broad permissions in the release-tools cluster and replaces it with an explicit, reviewable policy bundle:
- New `ClusterRole` + namespace-scoped bindings for `release-signing-operator`
- Separation-of-duties: operator can create/approve signing requests and observe jobs, but cannot read signing key material (Secrets) or mutate trust roots
- Policy tests to prevent accidental wildcard RBAC in this area

Context: offline bundle signing pipeline workstream (ENG-4822) + trust store/key rotation migration (ENG-4825). This is intended to be deployed alongside the signing service rollout in the release-tools cluster.

## Motivation / background
As part of...

title: Add audit export permissions + default role mappings (enterprise admin vs auditor)
preview:
Summary
This updates the rbac-policy-bundle to include new permissions required for SIEM-ready audit export configuration + operational visibility. It also updates default role mappings to keep least-privilege separation between enterprise admins (manage exports) and auditors (read-only + status).

text:
Summary
This updates the rbac-policy-bundle to include new permissions required for SIEM-ready audit export configuration + operational visibility. It also updates default role mappings to keep least-privilege separation between enterprise admins (manage exports) and auditors (read-only + status).

Context
The control plane and Console are introducing audit export CRUD + delivery status endpoints behind a beta feature flag. This PR updates the central policy bundle so permissions are standardized across Hosted and Dedicated. Private/on-prem roles are intentionally unchanged for beta.

Linked work
- ENG-5173 (RBAC permissions for export management)
- Control plane endpoints: redwood/pr-28792 (RBAC) + redwood/pr-28766 (config API) (FYI)

What changed
1) New permission set (names + semantics)
- audit_exports.read
 - Allows viewing export configurations (destination type, enabled/disabled...

title: Add Private deployment operator + auditor role templates (env/cluster scoped)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a set of built-in RBAC v2 role templates intended for Redwood Private deployments, focused on common operator and auditor workflows. The templates are explicitly scoped (environment and cluster) and are designed to support least-privilege, separation of duties, and evidence-friendly auditabilit...
text: Summary: This PR adds a set of built-in RBAC v2 role templates intended for Redwood Private deployments, focused on common operator and auditor workflows. The templates are explicitly scoped (environment and cluster) and are designed to support least-privilege, separation of duties, and evidence-friendly auditability. Context/motivation: Private customers (and internal pilots) have asked for clear “day-2 operations” roles (upgrade, health checks, log viewing) and separate read-only/audit roles that can export audit logs without granting mutation privileges. We also saw multiple support cases where Dedicated/Private admins were blocked due to missing template permissions or where customers over-granted Org Admin to solve an operator need (SUP-48288, SUP-48219). This PR is part of ENG-18444 (role template generator + templates) and aligns with the scope model decisions being rolled into...

title: Add rollout-approver role + permissions for Hosted canary rollouts
preview: Summary: Introduces a dedicated `rollout-approver` role and updates permission mappings to support the Hosted canary rollout workflow GA. This role is intended for orgs that want separation of duties: engineers can draft/create rollouts, but a smaller set of users must approve step changes (or rollback) when approva...
text:
Summary: Introduces a dedicated `rollout-approver` role and updates permission mappings to support the Hosted canary rollout workflow GA. This role is intended for orgs that want separation of duties: engineers can draft/create rollouts, but a smaller set of users must approve step changes (or rollback) when approval gates are enabled by policy.

Context / why: The canary workflow adds new actions that need explicit RBAC coverage: create rollout, start rollout, progress step (increase canary %), approve gated step, pause, rollback/cancel. Product + Security requested an explicit approver role rather than overloading existing admin roles, and Support requested clearer error messages when customers lack permissions.

What’s in this PR: Adds new role definition `rollout_approver` (exposed as `rollout-approver` in Console) and wires the following permissions: - `rollouts.read`: view rollo...

title: Generate built-in role templates from YAML + linting + versioned release artifacts
preview: Context: As part of RBAC v2 we want built-in role templates (Org Admin, Security Admin, Read-only Auditor, Deployment Operator, etc.) to be (a) human-reviewable, (b) lintable in CI, and (c) published as versioned artifacts that services (Control Plane, Console, Private installer) can consume deterministically. This ...
text: Context: As part of RBAC v2 we want built-in role templates (Org Admin, Security Admin, Read-only Auditor, Deployment Operator, etc.) to be (a) human-reviewable, (b) lintable in CI, and (c) published as versioned artifacts that services (Control Plane, Console, Private installer) can consume deterministically. This PR introduces a YAML-first source of truth under policy_bundle/roles/*.yaml and a generator that produces the canonical JSON bundle consumed by platform components. It also adds a lint/validation suite to enforce naming/versioning conventions, uniqueness constraints, and cross-scope safety checks, and formalizes an artifact versioning scheme tied to the repo release version. Summary of changes: (1) New role template YAML schema (name, description, scopes, included_permissions, excluded_permissions, constraints) with examples for common templates; (2) Generator CLI (scripts/...
